title: How to inject the male cheek & create a masculine result.

excerpt: So we're going to start the top and work our way down and just for the benefit of the camera male cheek we're creating a sense of bone muscle athleticism usually and what we identified in the consultation is this naturally there's a fat pad here which depletes laterally and if I …

segments.text: so we're going to start the top and work our way down and just for the benefit of the camera male cheek we're creating a sense of bone muscle athleticism usually and what we identified in the consultation is this naturally there's a fat pad here which depletes laterally and if I can just continue that slightly and great the sense of the zygoma pushing through here that that's going to create a more masculine angled appearance so that is my goal it's not a lot of injections we don't need to over treat or put large volumes into men typically so my two key points would be angle of zygoma which I identify with anterior surface lateral surface and looking for the meeting point and that is usually on a line in a man between the ear and the corner of the nose just this point here [Music] normally do all these drawings
that's key and this is key [Music] thank you [Music]
so same principle here I see another little tiny flat point we're going to just merge those two in [Music] thank you [Music] [Applause] [Music] foreign

foreign [Music] what I've been doing is just blending in the cheek here I've softened the lateral logic Junction and as I've built that cheek up I can still see a little flat point just here so I'm just doing a little tweak [Music]

transcript: so we're going to start the top and work our way down and just for the benefit of the camera male cheek we're creating a sense of bone muscle athleticism usually and what we identified in the consultation is this naturally there's a fat pad here which depletes laterally and if I can just continue that slightly and great the sense of the zygoma pushing through here that that's going to create a more masculine angled appearance so that is my goal it's not a lot of injections we don't need to over treat or put large volumes into men typically so my two key points would be angle of zygoma which I identify with anterior surface lateral surface and looking for the meeting point and that is usually on a line in a man between the ear and the corner of the nose just this point here [Music] normally do all these drawings that's key and this is key [Music] thank you [Music] so same principle here I see another little tiny flat point we're going to just merge those two in [Music] thank you [Music] [Applause] [Music] foreign [Music] thank you [Music] thank you [Music] foreign [Music] foreign [Music] what I've been doing is just blending in the cheek here I've softened the lateral logic Junction and as I've built that cheek up I can still see a little flat point just here so I'm just doing a little tweak [Music] [Music] thank you [Music] foreign [Music]
